description:
This diagram, titled "Synthetic Aperture geometric representation", illustrates key concepts of electromagnetic wave polarization and scattering relevant to Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR).

Section (i), "Locus of an elliptically polarized wave", shows a 3D coordinate system (x, y, z). An electric field propagates along the z-axis, forming a helical path. The electric field vector traces an ellipse in the "Transverse plane" (x-y plane).

Section (ii), "Typical polarizations", presents three 2D x-y coordinate systems demonstrating different polarization types:
(a) "Horizontal polarization, Vertical polarization" shows arrows along the x-axis labeled "H" and along the y-axis labeled "V", representing linear horizontal and vertical polarizations.
(b) "Linear 45 degree polarization, Linear -45 degree polarization" shows arrows along diagonal axes, labeled "Lin. 45°" and "Lin. -45°", representing linear diagonal polarizations.
(c) "Left circular polarization, Right circular polarization" shows a circular path. A dashed arrow labeled "L" indicates counter-clockwise rotation for Left circular polarization, and a solid arrow labeled "R" indicates clockwise rotation for Right circular polarization.

Section (iii), "Scattering with respect to polarization", illustrates how radar waves interact with a forest (trees on ground) under different polarization combinations:
(a) "HH" (Horizontal transmit, Horizontal receive) shows incident horizontally polarized (H) waves (red arrows) scattering from the trees and ground, and returning as horizontally polarized (H) waves (green arrows).
(b) "HV and VH" (Horizontal transmit, Vertical receive; or Vertical transmit, Horizontal receive) shows incident horizontally polarized (H) waves (red arrows) scattering from the trees and ground, and returning as vertically polarized (V) waves (green arrows), demonstrating cross-polarization.
(c) "VV" (Vertical transmit, Vertical receive) shows incident vertically polarized (V) waves (red arrows) scattering from the trees and ground, and returning as vertically polarized (V) waves (green arrows).
